attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- optimize: Fully autonomous RVV optimization loop for libnn functions. Target VPU, toolchain, build flags, FPGA board, and hardware profile all come from config.toml — no hardcoded paths or CPU names. Runs a 7-step FSM — validate, select, analyze, implement, verify, measure, learn — then loops back to analyze with new insights. No human intervention required.
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.command:
python3 -c "
import re
with open('ROADMAP.md') as f:
    lines = f.readlines()
out = []
for line in lines:
    # Match data rows: start with '| <num>' and have at least 6 pipes already
    if re.match(r'^\| \d+\s+\|', line):
        line = line.rstrip('\n').rstrip()
        if not line.endswith('|'):
            line += ' |'
        line = line + '                        |                 |\n'
    out.append(line)
with open('ROADMAP.md','w') as f:
    f.writelines(out)
"
input.description: Append two empty columns to each data row

input.description: Check what files changed in latest commit

content.content:
commit be791370cda8d2252607030220292310481e5be5
Author: Nick Hung <nick@andestech.com>
Date:   Tue Apr 28 07:35:36 2026 +0800

    [opt/rvv] conv_HWC_s8_asym_bias_any: SW-pipeline + peephole tuning (1.0716x)
    
    nn_mat_mult_kernel_s8_offset_unroll4 hot path (rounds 1-6 cumulative on AX45MPV
    V1024_8MB FPGA, baseline 1,125,457 cyc -> 1,050,251 cyc, 1.0716x cum):
    
    - r1-r4: hoist vle32 loads above slideup chain; peel first iter and
      interleave 8 vle8 with 16 vand to hide D$ latency on the contri/shift/scale
      loads driving the requantize tail.
    - r5: SW-pipelined inner vd4dots loop (lines 1820-1937) -- unroll-by-2 with
      alternating buffers V16-V23 / V24-V31, dual-issuing vle8 (Phase B loads)
      with 16 vd4dots (Phase A computes on prev-iter's V24-V31). Steady-state
      cycles/phase = max(VLSU, VMAC) = 16 cyc instead of serial 32 cyc.
    - r6: two peepholes -- batch all vmax then all vmin in the 4-channel
      requantize tail (4-instr RAW gap hides ~3-4 cyc VALU latency vs the 1-instr
      gap of the alternating order); merge the vsetvli vl=1 used to zero V31 into
      the vsetvli vl=num_col_a covering the vredsum chain (vand at e32 m1 is 1 cyc
      regardless of VL; vredsum reads only V31[0]).
    
    Also split nn_dup_s8 into LMUL-tuned variants (m1 for short channel-dim copies,
    m4 for the inner full-row copy in the kernel-y inner loop). The original used
    implicit max LMUL which forced spill in the short-vl case; m1/m4 picks save
    ~3-5% on the im2col hot path.
    
    3/3 t_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any sub-tests pass on FPGA-measured binary.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 Include/riscv_nn_support.h                         |  36 ++++
 .../riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any.c     |  14 +-
 .../nn_mat_mult_kernel_s8_offset.c                 | 239 ++++++++++++++++-----
 3 files changed, 224 insertions(+), 65 deletions(-)

lines: | #   | Status | Function                                 | Cycles    | Opt_Cycles | Speedup | current_baseline_cycle | uarch_Opt_cycle |

content.text: Updated row 1 from commit be791370: baseline 1,125,457 → uarch-opt 1,050,251 cyc (1.0716x).

input.command:
python3 -c "
import re
with open('ROADMAP.md') as f:
    lines = f.readlines()
out = []
for line in lines:
    if line.startswith('| #'):
        line = line.rstrip('\n').rstrip()
        if not line.endswith('|'):
            line += ' |'
        line = line + ' uarch_speedup |\n'
    elif line.startswith('|---'):
        line = line.rstrip('\n').rstrip()
        if not line.endswith('|'):
            line += ' |'
        line = line + '---------------|\n'
    elif re.match(r'^\| \d+\s+\|', line):
        # parse the last two cycle columns
        line = line.rstrip('\n').rstrip()
        if not line.endswith('|'):
            line += ' |'
        # split on |
        parts = [p for p in line.split('|')]
        # parts[0]='' parts[-1]='' (empty before/after edges)
        # data fields between
        # columns are: '', #, status, function, cycles, opt_cycles, speedup, baseline, uarch_opt, ''
        try:
            baseline_s = parts[-3].strip().replace(',','')
            uarch_s = parts[-2].strip().replace(',','')
            if baseline_s and uarch_s:
                baseline = float(baseline_s)
                uarch = float(uarch_s)
                speedup = baseline / uarch
                line = line + f' {speedup:.4f}x       |\n'
            else:
                line = line + '               |\n'
        except Exception:
            line = line + '               |\n'
    out.append(line)
with open('ROADMAP.md','w') as f:
    f.writelines(out)
"

python3 -c "
import re
with open('ROADMAP.md') as f:
    content = f.read()
content = re.sub(r'\| \[[x~\-]\]    \|', '| [ ]    |', content)
with open('ROADMAP.md','w') as f:
    f.write(content)
"

# Instruciton
Use /optimize harness skill to optimize each function, focus on the micro-architecture tuning using 
docs/wiki/general/ax45mpv-v1024-8mb-pipeline-tuning.md
Including instruction scheduling/interleaving, SW-pipelining, Peephole optimization, 
Resolve VRF port conflict, dual issue constraint, ...etc
1. Record the current cycle in current_baseline_cycle and the optimized version in Uarch_Opt_Cycles.
2. Run /clear before the next function.


Status legend: `[ ]` todo, `[x]` done, `[~]` in progress, `[-]` skipped

Baseline source: `~/work/baseline_perf.csv`

---

| 2   | [ ]    | conv_HWC_u8_u8_s8_sym_bias_fast          | 2,297,385 | 586,051    | 3.92x   |                        |                 |               |
